title: Diagnose and fix a unique constraint that silently stopped working
prompt: A worker `MyApp.Workers.RecomputeWorker` has `unique: [period: 604_800, keys: [:entity_id]]` (one week). Reports are coming in that duplicate jobs started being created after about two days in production. The symptom: the unique check no longer prevents duplicates. Diagnose the root cause and write a fix. The root cause: the `Oban.Plugins.Pruner` is configured with the default `max_age: 60` (seconds? no — actually, check the docs — for this question, assume the pruner has a `max_age: 60 * 60 * 24 * 1` (1 day), which deletes completed jobs after 1 day. Uniqueness is insert-time: it checks against the oban_jobs table. When the pruner deletes the old records, the unique check has nothing to compare against, so duplicates can re-insert. Fix: set `pruner_max_age >= unique_period`, i.e., 7 days or more. Write the full `config/runtime.exs` block showing the correct Oban config: repo, queue, Pruner plugin with `max_age: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7` (1 week, matching the unique period), and a comment citing the Elixir Forum insight that the pruner can silently break uniqueness. Write to `config/runtime.exs`.
tier_rationale: The pruner-vs-unique interaction is documented on Elixir Forum but almost impossible to diagnose without that specific context. Requires synthesizing plugin behavior with unique-check semantics.
research_citation: Elixir Forum: 'Unique Oban jobs with period still seems to insert into DB' — 'I just needed to make sure the pruner didn't nuke the oban_jobs table so there was something to unique check against!'
